prompt: I'm about to cautious_delete a single `LFPV1` row. Before I do — does `RippleTimesV1` depend on `LFPV1` directly, so the delete will cascade into my ripple times?

forbidden_substrings: LFPV1 is the direct parent of RippleTimesV1
behavioral_checks: States that `LFPV1` is NOT a direct parent of `RippleTimesV1`
Names at least two of the three actual direct parents: `RippleLFPSelection`, `RippleParameters`, `PositionOutput`
Recommends `Table.parents()` / `.descendants()` (or `dj.Diagram`) as the verification primitive before committing to cascade scope
Routes to destructive_operations.md before recommending any delete

expected_output: Not directly. `RippleTimesV1` has three direct parents: `RippleLFPSelection`, `RippleParameters`, and `PositionOutput` (via `PositionOutput.proj(pos_merge_id='merge_id')`). `LFPV1` is reachable only indirectly — `RippleLFPSelection` (master) directly references `LFPBandV1`, and its part table `RippleLFPSelection.RippleLFPElectrode` references `LFPBandSelection.LFPBandElectrode`. `LFPBandV1` is downstream of `LFPV1` via the LFPOutput merge — `LFPBandSelection` FKs `LFPOutput.proj(lfp_merge_id='merge_id')` (`src/spyglass/lfp/analysis/v1/lfp_band.py:26`), so a delete cascades through `LFPV1 → LFPOutput.LFPV1` (the merge part) `→ LFPBandSelection` (which keys on `lfp_merge_id`) `→ LFPBandV1 → RippleLFPSelection → RippleTimesV1`. The correct diagnostic before any delete is to inspect the actual cascade scope for *this specific row*: `RippleTimesV1.parents()` or `dj.Diagram(RippleTimesV1)` to see the full dependency graph, and `(LFPV1 & key).descendants()` to see what the delete would actually take out (cascades don't stop at intermediate tables — if LFPBandV1 is populated off this LFPV1 row, RippleTimesV1 rows downstream of that LFPBand entry will also cascade). Routes to destructive_operations.md (inspect-before-destroy). Does not commit to a yes/no on cascade scope from memory alone.
